channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Macron met with anger and frustration over cyclone response during French leader's visit to Mayotte
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron faced widespread frustration and anger from residents of Mayotte during his visit to the Indian Ocean archipelago.
item.dc:creator: Sam Mednick, Adrienne Surprenant And Sylvie Corbet, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Sri Lanka's navy rescues 102 Rohingya refugees found adrift aboard a fishing trawler
item.description: COLOMBO, Sri Lanka (AP) — Sri Lanka's navy rescued more than 100 people believed to be Rohingya refugees who were found adrift aboard a fishing trawler off the island nation, a navy spokesman said Friday.
item.dc:creator: BHARATHA MALLAWARACHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Diplomatic spat erupts after Hungary gives asylum to Polish politician suspected of corruption
item.description: Hungary has given political asylum to a Polish opposition politician who is wanted on suspicions of corruption.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Gera, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Malaysia agrees to resume 'no find, no fee' hunt for flight MH370, 10 years after plane disappeared
item.description: KUALA LUMPUR, Malaysia (AP) — Malaysia's government has agreed in principle to accept a second “no find, no fee” proposal from a U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: GOP seeks ‘maximum pressure’ on Iran with nuclear clock ticking
item.description: Republican lawmakers want a return to "maximum pressure" on Iran in the second Trump administration and are widely open to the idea of strikes on Iranian nuclear sites if it’s the only way to prevent Tehran from wielding nuclear weapons.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: 8 dead, 18 rescued as speedboat carrying migrants capsizes near Greek island of Rhodes
item.description: ATHENS, Greeece (AP) — A speedboat carrying migrants capsized Friday morning off the eastern Greek island of Rhodes, leaving eight dead, Greek authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Taiwan lawmakers clash after party members break into legislature to occupy speaker's chair
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — Lawmakers clashed in Taiwan's legislature Friday when members of a leading political party who had broken into the building overnight to occupy the speaker's chair tussled with members of another major party who forced their way in to evict them.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Iran expands weaponization capabilities critical for employing nuclear bomb
item.description: A new report presented by the National Council of Resistance of Iran claims Tehran has expanded attempts to secretly develop weaponization capabilities for its nuclear program.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: For thousands of Jews, Israel still doesn't feel safe after the Oct. 7 attacks. So they're leaving
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Leaving Israel is easier, Shira Z. Carmel thinks, by saying it's just for now. But she knows better. For the Israeli-born singer and an increasing number of relatively well-off Israelis, the Oct.
item.dc:creator: LAURIE KELLMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korean investigators seek to question reluctant president over martial law
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korean investigators on Friday again sought to question impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol over his Dec. 3 declaration of martial law, summoning him for questioning on Christmas Day despite his repeated refusal to cooperate.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gisele Pelicot's Australian supporters are moved that their French heroine wore an Aboriginal scarf
item.description: A group of Gisele Pelicot’s Australian admirers say they're moved that the victim in France’s notorious drugging-and-rape case has acknowledged her distant supporters Down Under by wearing a scarf adorned with Aboriginal art.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: What to know about Vanuatu, the Pacific island nation struggling to recover from a massive quake
item.description: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — A search expanded on Thursday for those killed or trapped alive by an earthquake in the Pacific island nation of Vanuatu, as rescue workers arriving from abroad fanned out through the capital, Port Vila, encountering flattened buildings that had not yet been searched.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US diplomats in Damascus for first time in more than 10 years following fall of Assad regime
item.description: U.S. officials are in Syria with goals to advance Aqaba principles and seek information on American citizens who disappeared under the Assad regime.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Turkey and Russia engage in delicate maneuvers over Syria after Assad’s downfall
item.description: The rapid downfall of Syrian leader Bashar Assad has touched off a new round of delicate geopolitical maneuvering between Russia's Vladimir Putin and Turkey's Recep Tayyip Erdogan.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden to travel to Italy for meetings with pope, Meloni in January
item.description: President Biden will travel to Italy in January for meetings with Pope Francis, Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni and Italian President Sergio Mattarella. The trip, from Jan. 9 to 12, will likely be his last foreign trip as president before President-elect Trump is sworn in on Jan. 20.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Pentagon says the number of US troops in Syria is much higher than previously reported
item.description: The Pentagon said Thursday that there are about 2,000 U.S. Troops in Syria, a number that is more than double the 900 troops reported previously by the DoD for months.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pentagon doubled the number of US troops in Syria 'months' ahead of Assad’s fall
item.description: The U.S. for months has had more than 2,000 troops inside Syria, more than double the 900 troops the Defense Department previously said are on the ground there, the Pentagon’s top spokesperson revealed Thursday. The increased force presence has “been going on for a while,” with press secretary Maj.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: 13 die as an Indian navy speedboat crashes into a passenger ferry off Mumbai
item.description: At least 13 people were killed after an Indian navy speedboat crashed into a ferry that was carrying more than 100 people; the ferry was on its way to a tourist destination off Mumbai.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Blinken says 'pressure is on Hamas' to move forward with ceasefire in Gaza
item.description: Secretary of State Antony Blinken said the pressure is on the Palestinian militant group Hamas to move forward with the ceasefire in the Gaza Strip, arguing the group’s support from its allies in the region has dwindled in recent months.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Putin ‘promises’ to ask Assad for help in finding Austin Tice following letter from mother
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin promised to ask former Syrian President Assad for help in obtaining information on American journalist Austin Tice after he was taken prisoner 12 years ago.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Putin boasts of gains in Ukraine at annual news conference meant to reinforce his authority
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — President Vladimir Putin boasted that his military operation in Ukraine has strengthened Russia and denied that the ouster of key ally Bashar Assad in Syria had hurt Moscow’s prestige, as he took questions at his annual news conference and call-in show Thursday.
item.dc:creator: VLADIMIR ISACHENKOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Putin says Russia ready to compromise with Trump on Ukraine war
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin said he was ready to make a compromise regarding ending the war in Ukraine in possible talks with U.S. President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Syrian villagers near the Golan Heights say Israeli forces are banning them from their fields
item.description: Israeli forces have set up a position in an abandoned Syrian army base in the village of Maariyah and prevented local farmers from accessing their fields, residents said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: GHAITH ALSAYED and HUSSEIN MALLA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 5 takeaways from Putin's annual press conference
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin talked for more than three hours during an annual news conference in Moscow on Thursday, discussing key topics including the war in Ukraine, his interest in dialogue with President-elect Trump and an openness to asking about detained journalist Austin Tice in Syria.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Number of dead and missing still unclear as first aid arrives in quake-hit Pacific nation of Vanuatu
item.description: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — Amid the havoc wrought by a violent earthquake two days earlier, Ivan Oswald and his staff prepared for lunchtime service Thursday at Nambawan Cafe, on an idyllic stretch of Vanuatu’s waterfront.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: German lawmakers back plan to protect supreme court against meddling by authoritarians, extremists
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — German lawmakers on Thursday approved legislation designed to protect the country's highest court against possible future manipulation or obstruction by authoritarian or extremist politicians.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Palestinian Authority under pressure amid rising resistance, popularity of Iran-backed terror groups
item.description: Palestinian Authority’s crackdown on terrorists intensifies, but efforts to regain control of the city and surrounding areas face fierce resistance.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Italy's Salvini faces verdict for blocking migrants at sea. Case weighs limits of stemming migration
item.description: MILAN (AP) — Italy’s deputy premier, Matteo Salvini, is sounding defiant as he awaits Friday’s verdict in Sicily for blocking some 100 migrants at sea on a humanitarian rescue boat in 2019 when he was interior minister.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Frenchman found guilty in horrific rape trial that shocked the world
item.description: A Frenchman accused of drugging his wife of 50 years and inviting strangers over to rape her – while he filmed the assaults – was found guilty on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China skirts US efforts to stiff-arm CCP interference by bolstering state, local relations
item.description: China is skirting U.S. efforts to block its interference in areas ranging from trade to national security by cozying up with state officials to foster relations and keep its influence active in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pro-Palestinian activists storm basketball court during Israeli team’s game against France
item.description: Anti-Israel agitators disrupted an Israeli-French basketball game Wednesday, waving Palestinian flags and sparking chaos in the stands.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden envoy: Ukraine's future in NATO now in Trump's hands
item.description: A senior aide to President Biden said that the administration will leave up to the incoming Trump administration the next steps on Ukraine’s path to NATO membership, despite internal discussions on whether to push for the alliance to issue an invitation for Kyiv in the lame-duck session.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: French high court upholds ex-president's corruption conviction
item.description: A Court of Cassation decision upheld the corruption conviction of former French President Nicolas Sarkozy, who now faces a year in prison.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 51 percent oppose Trump tariffs on Mexico, Canada and China: Poll
item.description: Slightly more than half, 51 percent, of voters in the U.S. oppose President-elect Trump's plan to impose tariffs on imported goods from Mexico, Canada and China, a Quinnipiac University poll revealed.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill
